body: Hey, I visited Sunflower Montessori last week and I really liked it. They have an opening for Mia in the fall 3-year-old class. The hours work with both our schedules and it's only a few blocks from your place too. Can we go together this week to check it out before the spot fills?
I'm not really sure about that place. I don't know anything about it.

I can send you their website and the info packet they gave me. It's accredited and the teachers have been there for years. Really stable environment. Would Thursday afternoon work to go look together?

I talked to my attorney and she said that per the parenting plan, major educational decisions require my agreement. You don't just get to pick a school because you toured it. That's not how this works.
That's exactly why I'm asking you to come look with me — so we decide together. I'm not enrolling her without you. I'm trying to include you in this.

I've been looking into schools on my side and I don't think Montessori is the right approach for Mia. My mother was a teacher and she agrees. I think a structured program is better for her at this age. I'm going to look at Bright Beginnings this week.
Fine, I'm open to looking at Bright Beginnings too. Can we visit both and then compare? The Sunflower spot won't hold forever but let's look at both this week if possible.
I enrolled Mia at Bright Beginnings this morning. They had one spot left and I wasn't going to lose it. She starts September 4th. I'll forward you the paperwork.
